generated_headline: P&G PAID $45 MILLION FOR FIXING LAUNDRY DETERGENT PRICES IN EUROPE — ITS TIDE PODS SENT 10,000 CHILDREN TO EMERGENCY ROOMS IN A SINGLE YEAR
executive_summary: The Procter & Gamble Company is one of the world's largest consumer goods companies, operating across beauty, grooming, health, fabric, home care, and baby/feminine care categories with approximately $84 billion in annual revenue. In 2011, the European Commission fined P&G €211 million (approximately $280 million) as part of a broader €315 million fine against P&G and Unilever for fixing laundry detergent prices across Europe. P&G's Tide Pods — the concentrated laundry detergent pods — were linked to approximately 10,000 calls to poison control centers and emergency room visits in 2017 alone, primarily involving children who ingested the colorful pods. P&G has also been subject to significant greenwashing scrutiny for its environmental claims.

labor.summary: P&G employs approximately 107,000 people globally. The company has documented diversity and inclusion commitments. No major systematic labor violations have been identified as a primary concern.

environmental.summary: P&G has been criticized by environmental groups for greenwashing — making environmental claims that independent auditors find overstated. The company's palm oil supply chain has documented deforestation concerns. P&G committed to sustainable palm oil sourcing but independent monitoring has found gaps in supply chain traceability.

political.summary: P&G has lobbied against chemical safety reforms, environmental disclosure requirements, and product ingredient transparency regulations. The company funds trade associations that lobby against regulations affecting its product categories.

legal.summary: 2011: EU Commission fines P&G €211 million for coordinating laundry detergent prices with Unilever across multiple European markets. Tide Pods: P&G faced regulatory pressure and class action litigation following the pediatric poisoning surge in 2017; implemented child-resistant packaging upgrades. No criminal enforcement actions against P&G executives have been documented.

tax.summary: P&G uses international IP holding structures across its global brand portfolio. The company has benefited from TCJA provisions. EU state aid investigations have examined multinational consumer goods IP structures, including P&G's Belgian operations. No criminal tax enforcement has been documented.

executives.summary: CEO Jon Moeller leads P&G. No executive was personally charged in the EU price-fixing case — the penalty was at the corporate level. P&G has maintained continuity of leadership through its compliance issues.

connections.summary: The P&G-Unilever European price-fixing coordination represents a documented cartel between the two largest consumer goods companies operating in European markets — companies that together dominate household cleaning products, beauty, and personal care globally.

allegations.disclaimer: The following are allegations and unproven claims documented in credible sources but not adjudicated.
allegations.summary: Environmental advocates have alleged that P&G's 'responsible sourcing' certifications for palm oil do not provide meaningful assurance of deforestation-free supply chains given the fragmented structure of palm oil smallholder farming and the limitations of current certification bodies.

health_record.summary: Tide Pods: approximately 10,000 poison control center calls and emergency room visits in 2017 involving children who ingested the concentrated detergent pods. The colorful, candy-like appearance of pods contributed to pediatric ingestion rates. P&G implemented packaging and formulation changes following regulatory pressure. The CPSC and AAP issued warnings about Tide Pods specifically.

cheaper: Store brand laundry detergent — often manufactured by P&G or Unilever; same chemistry, lower price
Powder detergent — lower packaging volume, lower cost per load, no pod ingestion risk
healthier: Powder detergent in cardboard packaging — no pod ingestion risk; lower plastic packaging
Method, Seventh Generation — marketed as plant-based with different environmental claims
diy: Tide Pods should be stored in locked or high cabinets out of reach of children — the CPSC child-resistant packaging does not prevent access if left accessible
DIY laundry powder: washing soda + borax + bar soap is significantly cheaper per load than any branded product

timeline.event: EU Commission fines P&G €211M and Unilever for laundry detergent price-fixing across multiple European markets
Tide Pods launched in US; bright colors and concentrated formula create pediatric ingestion risk
Approximately 10,000 children involved in Tide Pod poisoning incidents; CPSC and AAP issue warnings; viral 'Tide Pod Challenge' adds teen cases
P&G implements child-resistant upgrades to Tide Pod packaging following regulatory pressure

community_impact.displacement: Pediatric Tide Pod poisoning incidents affected children and families across the US — an entirely preventable harm from a product designed without adequate consideration of its appeal to children.
community_impact.price_illusion: P&G products are priced as premium consumer goods with strong environmental and safety marketing. The €211 million price-fixing settlement established that detergent prices in Europe were artificially elevated through coordination with Unilever. The premium consumers paid was partly a function of cartel pricing.
community_impact.tax_math: P&G's international IP structures minimize tax obligations on global brand royalties. The EU price-fixing fine is tax-deductible as a business expense.
community_impact.wealth_velocity: P&G is broadly held by institutional investors. Revenue from laundry detergent priced above competitive levels during the cartel period concentrated in shareholder returns.

who_benefited.how: Above-market laundry detergent prices across European markets during the coordination period

who_paid.how: Above-market detergent prices during the P&G-Unilever price-fixing coordination
Pediatric poisoning from Tide Pod ingestion; hospital visits; lasting effects in some cases

cost_absorption.the_gap: P&G coordinated laundry detergent prices with Unilever across Europe. The EU fined them €211 million. They designed Tide Pods to look like candy. 10,000 children went to poison control or the emergency room in one year. P&G implemented child-resistant packaging after the regulatory pressure. They still make Tide Pods.
